cities.name: Vientiane
cities.notes: Patuxai victory arch, Pha That Luang stupa and slow Mekong evenings

cities.name: Luang Prabang
cities.notes: UNESCO old town with alms-giving monks at dawn and Kuang Si Falls

cities.name: Vang Vieng
cities.notes: Karst mountain backdrop, Nam Song River tubing and hot air balloons

cities.name: Pakse
cities.notes: Gateway to 4000 Islands and Bolaven Plateau coffee country

Vientiane.name: Vientiane
Vientiane.note: Two days covers the capital well; add a third only if you want a slower arrival or more café time by the Mekong.

activities.name: Pha That Luang
activities.tip: Go early for softer light and cooler temple grounds.

activities.name: Patuxai Victory Gate
activities.tip: Climb for the Lane Xang Boulevard view before sunset haze.

meals: Talat Sao Morning Market noodle stalls

days.theme: Riverside Life, Markets & Easy Day Sights

activities.name: COPE Visitor Centre
activities.tip: Leave a small donation if the exhibit resonates with you.

activities.name: Buddha Park (Xieng Khuan)
activities.tip: Budget about ₹336 for a shared tuk-tuk plus ₹20 entry.

meals: Nam Phu Fountain night market stalls

Vang Vieng.name: Vang Vieng
Vang Vieng.note: Keep two full days for the lagoons and river activities; a third day only helps if weather delays ballooning or kayaking.

activities.name: Tham Poukham (Blue Lagoon 1)
activities.tip: Carry cash for the lagoon entry and a torch if you plan to continue into the cave.

activities.name: Torch rental for Tham Poukham cave
Tham Nam Water Cave
activities.tip: This usually covers the cave tube and bridge fee together.

activities.name: Bicycle rental through the rice fields
River tubing on the Nam Song
activities.tip: Expect a refundable tube deposit of about ₹240 on top.

activities.name: Hot air balloon over the karst valley
activities.tip: Book the day before and choose sunrise if the wind forecast looks better.

activities.name: Kayaking tour on the Nam Song
SAE Lao Organic Farm visit
activities.tip: The farm visit is free; pay only if you stay for lunch.

activities.name: Sunset by the river bridges
activities.tip: Avoid homemade spirits or “happy” drinks and stick to sealed beverages only.

Luang Prabang.name: Luang Prabang
Luang Prabang.note: Three days is the sweet spot; add a fourth only if you want a slow-boat arrival, more cafés, or another waterfall day.

days.theme: Royal Temples, Alms Ceremony & Phou Si Sunset

activities.name: Tak Bat / Sai Bat alms ceremony
activities.tip: Observe respectfully from a distance unless you have arranged proper offerings.

activities.name: Phou Si Hill
activities.tip: Go at sunset; after the ticket booth closes, access may be effectively free.

meals: Luang Prabang Night Market food stalls

days.theme: Kuang Si Falls, Bears & Craft Traditions

activities.name: Kuang Si Falls
activities.tip: Add roughly ₹180–₹504 for transport depending on whether you take the green bus or a shared tuk-tuk.

activities.name: Bear Rescue Centre at Kuang Si
Kuang Si treetop walk or zipline
Ock Pop Tok Living Craft Centre
activities.tip: The basic visit is free; workshops cost extra.

activities.name: Lao Red Cross sauna and massage

days.theme: Pak Ou Caves, Markets & Lao Culture

activities.name: Pak Ou Caves shared boat trip
activities.tip: This usually bundles the boat and cave entry; confirm if your operator also stops at Ban Xang Hai.

Pakse.name: Pakse
Pakse.note: Use Pakse as your southern Laos hub; if Si Phan Don matters more than waterfalls, swap the second day for a Don Det extension and overnight farther south.

activities.name: Wat Phou day trip
activities.tip: Add about ₹1,200–₹1,600 for a taxi or shared tour from Pakse if you are not renting a motorbike.

activities.name: Phou Salao Golden Buddha viewpoint
activities.tip: The hilltop view is best at sunset; the extra spend is only for a tuk-tuk or taxi ride.

activities.name: Pakse Xe Don and Mekong riverside walk
Bolaven Plateau day tour (Tad Fane, Tad Yuang, coffee estates)
activities.tip: This is the easiest way to cover the waterfalls and coffee tastings in one day.

activities.name: Motorbike loop alternative
activities.tip: Rent only if you are comfortable with wet roads and village traffic.

activities.name: Sinouk or Dao Coffee tasting stop
Optional Don Det swap via Ban Nakasang boat connection
activities.tip: This covers the Pakse minibus and the short boat; withdraw cash first because Don Det has no ATM.

extras: Indian passport holders should budget about ₹3,360 for an e-Visa or visa on arrival and complete the Laos online arrival form before landing.
November to February is the easiest weather window for this route; roads and river conditions become less predictable in the monsoon.
LCR train seats can sell out fast; if the app will not register with your number, book through a guesthouse or agent for roughly ₹336 extra.
Use Loca for airport and station rides in the north, but download offline maps before southern legs because coverage weakens around Don Det.
Ask for “bo sai sin” for no meat and “bo sai pa daek” for no fermented fish when ordering vegetarian Lao dishes.
Avoid homemade spirits and any “happy” food or drinks in Vang Vieng; stick to sealed commercial beverages only.
Do not plan around Irrawaddy dolphin sightings in Laos; the Laos population died out in 2022 and many older itineraries are outdated.
